Our method iteratively refines the training dataset by re-evaluating outputs, identifying high-scoring negative examples, and incorporating them into the training loop, that boosting model performance. Our evaluation on the MultiPL-E dataset demonstrates that our 13.4B parameter model outperforms a 33B model in code generation quality while being three times faster. Moreover, it achieves performance comparable to GPT-4 and surpasses it in one programming language. Comments: Published at ECIR 2025 Subjects: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Information Retrieval (cs.IR); Software Engineering (cs.SE) Cite as: arXiv:2504.09643 [cs.CL] (or arXiv:2504.09643v1 [cs.CL] for this version) https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2504.09643 Focus to learn more arXiv-issued DOI via DataCite (pending registration) Related DOI: https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-031-88714-7_21 Focus to learn more DOI(s) linking to related resources
zh

[NLP-76] Leverag ing Reasoning Model Answers to Enhance Non-Reasoning Model Capability

【速读】： 该论文试图解决如何利用高计算成本的推理密集型大语言模型（Reasoning-Intensive LLMs）的高质量输出，来提升低计算成本的非推理型大语言模型（Non-Reasoning LLMs）的能力。论文的关键解决方案是通过监督微调（Supervised Fine-Tuning, SFT）方法，将推理密集型模型生成的答案作为训练数据，系统性地改进非推理型模型的问答能力，并通过在多个基准测试中的实验验证了此方法的有效性和一致性。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09639
作者: Haotian Wang,Han Zhao,Shuaiting Chen,Xiaoyu Tian,Sitong Zhao,Yunjie Ji,Yiping Peng,Xiangang Li
机构: a-m-team
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Recent advancements in large language models (LLMs), such as DeepSeek-R1 and OpenAI-o1, have demonstrated the significant effectiveness of test-time scaling, achieving substantial performance gains across various benchmarks. These advanced models utilize deliberate “thinking” steps to systematically enhance answer quality. In this paper, we propose leveraging these high-quality outputs generated by reasoning-intensive models to improve less computationally demanding, non-reasoning models. We explore and compare methodologies for utilizing the answers produced by reasoning models to train and improve non-reasoning models. Through straightforward Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) experiments on established benchmarks, we demonstrate consistent improvements across various benchmarks, underscoring the potential of this approach for advancing the ability of models to answer questions directly.
zh

[NLP-77] Metropolis-Hastings Captioning Game: Knowledge Fusion of Vision Language Models via Decentralized Bayesian Inference

【速读】： 该论文试图解决多视觉-语言模型（Vision-Language Models, VLMs）融合过程中存在的推理成本高和架构约束的问题。解决方案的关键在于提出Metropolis-Hastings Captioning Game (MHCG)，通过一种类似语言游戏的去中心化贝叶斯推理过程，使两个VLM代理交替为图像生成描述并相互学习，从而实现知识融合，而无需增加额外的推理成本或受限于特定架构。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09620
作者: Yuta Matsui,Ryosuke Yamaki,Ryo Ueda,Seitaro Shinagawa,Tadahiro Taniguchi
机构: Ritsumeikan University (立命馆大学); The University of Tokyo (东京大学); Nara Institute of Science and Technology (奈良先端科学技术大学院大学); Kyoto University (京都大学)
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV); Multiagent Systems (cs.MA)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We propose the Metropolis-Hastings Captioning Game (MHCG), a method to fuse knowledge of multiple vision-language models (VLMs) by learning from each other. Although existing methods that combine multiple models suffer from inference costs and architectural constraints, MHCG avoids these problems by performing decentralized Bayesian inference through a process resembling a language game. The knowledge fusion process establishes communication between two VLM agents alternately captioning images and learning from each other. We conduct two image-captioning experiments with two VLMs, each pre-trained on a different dataset. The first experiment demonstrates that MHCG achieves consistent improvement in reference-free evaluation metrics. The second experiment investigates how MHCG contributes to sharing VLMs’ category-level vocabulary by observing the occurrence of the vocabulary in the generated captions.
zh

[NLP-78] Short-Path Prompting in LLM s: Analyzing Reasoning Instability and Solutions for Robust Performance

【速读】： 该论文试图解决在用户使用短路径提示（short-path prompts）的情况下，大型语言模型（Large Language Models, LLMs）在推理任务中的性能显著下降及不稳定性问题。当前最先进的LLMs通过指令微调（instruction-tuning）提供详细的链式思考（Chain-of-Thought, CoT）路径以增强推理能力，但当面对用户提供的简短提示时，这种能力大幅减弱甚至失效，尤其是在小学水平的问题上。为了解决这一冲突，论文提出了两种方法：一是基于指令引导的方法（instruction-guided approach），二是基于微调的方法（fine-tuning approach）。这两种方案旨在有效平衡指令遵循与推理准确性之间的权衡，从而提升LLMs在短路径提示下的推理性能。实验结果表明，这两种方法均取得了高精度，为理解当前模型在指令遵循与推理准确性之间的取舍提供了重要见解。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09586
作者: Zuoli Tang,Junjie Ou,Kaiqin Hu,Chunwei Wu,Zhaoxin Huan,Chilin Fu,Xiaolu Zhang,Jun Zhou,Chenliang Li
机构: Wuhan University (武汉大学); Ant Group (蚂蚁集团)
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Under review

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Recent years have witnessed significant progress in large language models’ (LLMs) reasoning, which is largely due to the chain-of-thought (CoT) approaches, allowing models to generate intermediate reasoning steps before reaching the final answer. Building on these advances, state-of-the-art LLMs are instruction-tuned to provide long and detailed CoT pathways when responding to reasoning-related questions. However, human beings are naturally cognitive misers and will prompt language models to give rather short responses, thus raising a significant conflict with CoT reasoning. In this paper, we delve into how LLMs’ reasoning performance changes when users provide short-path prompts. The results and analysis reveal that language models can reason effectively and robustly without explicit CoT prompts, while under short-path prompting, LLMs’ reasoning ability drops significantly and becomes unstable, even on grade-school problems. To address this issue, we propose two approaches: an instruction-guided approach and a fine-tuning approach, both designed to effectively manage the conflict. Experimental results show that both methods achieve high accuracy, providing insights into the trade-off between instruction adherence and reasoning accuracy in current models.
zh

[NLP-79] Reduction of Supervision for Biomedical Knowledge Discovery

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决因出版物数量激增和标注数据稀缺导致的知识发现难题，特别是在生物医学领域识别实体间语义关系时对标注数据高度依赖的问题。论文的关键在于提出了一组基于依存树（Dependency Trees）和注意力机制（Attention Mechanisms）的无监督算法，并结合多种点式二分类方法，逐步从弱监督过渡到完全无监督设置，评估其在处理带有噪声标签数据时的学习能力。通过在生物医学基准数据集上的评估，论文揭示了点式二分类技术在减少监督的情况下仍能有效应对噪声标签的鲁棒性，表明这些方法能够从弱监督顺利过渡到完全无监督场景。这种渐进式减少监督的方法为构建适应性强的知识发现系统提供了有价值的参考，推动了在标注数据有限条件下高效提取有用信息的数据高效方法的发展。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09582
作者: Christos Theodoropoulos,Andrei Catalin Coman,James Henderson,Marie-Francine Moens
机构: KU Leuven (鲁汶大学); IDIAP Research Institute (IDIAP 研究所)
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: Published as part of the PhD dissertation: Theodoropoulos, Christos, Marie-Francine Moens, and Matthew Blaschko. “Deep Learning Models for the Extraction of Knowledge from Text.” (2025)

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Knowledge discovery is hindered by the increasing volume of publications and the scarcity of extensive annotated data. To tackle the challenge of information overload, it is essential to employ automated methods for knowledge extraction and processing. Finding the right balance between the level of supervision and the effectiveness of models poses a significant challenge. While supervised techniques generally result in better performance, they have the major drawback of demanding labeled data. This requirement is labor-intensive and time-consuming and hinders scalability when exploring new domains. In this context, our study addresses the challenge of identifying semantic relationships between biomedical entities (e.g., diseases, proteins) in unstructured text while minimizing dependency on supervision. We introduce a suite of unsupervised algorithms based on dependency trees and attention mechanisms and employ a range of pointwise binary classification methods. Transitioning from weakly supervised to fully unsupervised settings, we assess the methods’ ability to learn from data with noisy labels. The evaluation on biomedical benchmark datasets explores the effectiveness of the methods. Our approach tackles a central issue in knowledge discovery: balancing performance with minimal supervision. By gradually decreasing supervision, we assess the robustness of pointwise binary classification techniques in handling noisy labels, revealing their capability to shift from weakly supervised to entirely unsupervised scenarios. Comprehensive benchmarking offers insights into the effectiveness of these techniques, suggesting an encouraging direction toward adaptable knowledge discovery systems, representing progress in creating data-efficient methodologies for extracting useful insights when annotated data is limited.
zh

[NLP-80] LLM s Can Achieve High-quality Simultaneous Machine Translation as Efficiently as Offline

【速读】： 该论文致力于解决在流式输入源句子场景下，解码器-only大语言模型（Decoder-only Large Language Models）在同时性机器翻译（Simultaneous Machine Translation, SiMT）任务中的效率与性能受限问题。论文的关键创新在于提出了一种新颖的方法，包括构建用于SiMT的有监督微调（Supervised Fine-Tuning, SFT）数据，以及新的训练和推理策略。具体而言，通过将源令牌和目标令牌重新排列成交错序列，并使用特殊分隔符根据不同的延迟需求组织输入输出流，使强大的大语言模型能够自适应地学习读写操作，同时保持高效的自回归解码能力。实验结果表明，即使在有限的SFT数据条件下，该方法在多种SiMT基准测试中达到了最先进的性能，并保留了离线翻译的原始能力，同时在文档级SiMT任务中表现出良好的泛化能力，无需特定的微调。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09570
作者: Biao Fu,Minpeng Liao,Kai Fan,Chengxi Li,Liang Zhang,Yidong Chen,Xiaodong Shi
机构: 未知
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:When the complete source sentence is provided, Large Language Models (LLMs) perform excellently in offline machine translation even with a simple prompt “Translate the following sentence from [src lang] into [tgt lang]:”. However, in many real scenarios, the source tokens arrive in a streaming manner and simultaneous machine translation (SiMT) is required, then the efficiency and performance of decoder-only LLMs are significantly limited by their auto-regressive nature. To enable LLMs to achieve high-quality SiMT as efficiently as offline translation, we propose a novel paradigm that includes constructing supervised fine-tuning (SFT) data for SiMT, along with new training and inference strategies. To replicate the token input/output stream in SiMT, the source and target tokens are rearranged into an interleaved sequence, separated by special tokens according to varying latency requirements. This enables powerful LLMs to learn read and write operations adaptively, based on varying latency prompts, while still maintaining efficient auto-regressive decoding. Experimental results show that, even with limited SFT data, our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance across various SiMT benchmarks, and preserves the original abilities of offline translation. Moreover, our approach generalizes well to document-level SiMT setting without requiring specific fine-tuning, even beyond the offline translation model.
zh

[NLP-81] Syzygy of Thoughts: Improving LLM CoT with the Minimal Free Resolution

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决复杂任务中单一线性推理路径（如 Chain-of-Thought, CoT）难以有效处理庞大解空间与模糊约束的问题。为应对这一挑战，论文提出了一种名为 Syzygy of Thoughts (SoT) 的新框架，其关键在于引入辅助且相互关联的推理路径，通过借鉴交换代数中的 Minimal Free Resolution (MFR) 方法，将原始复杂问题系统性分解为逻辑完整的最小子问题，同时保持核心问题特征并减少推理长度。SoT 捕捉更深层次的逻辑依赖关系，实现更具鲁棒性和结构化的推理能力。实验结果表明，SoT 在多个数据集和模型上的推理精度达到或超过了主流 CoT 标准，并通过与代数约束对齐的采样过程提升了大规模语言模型的推理时间可扩展性。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09566
作者: Chenghao Li,Chaoning Zhang,Yi Lu,Jiaquan Zhang,Qigan Sun,Xudong Wang,Jiwei Wei,Guoqing Wang,Yang Yang,Heng Tao Shen
机构: University of Electronic Science and Technology of China(电子科技大学), Chengdu, China; Capital Normal University(首都师范大学), Beijing, China; University of Liverpool(利物浦大学), Liverpool, United Kingdom; Kyung Hee University(庆熙大学), Yongin-si, Republic of Korea; Tongji University(同济大学), Shanghai, China
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting enhances the reasoning of large language models (LLMs) by decomposing problems into sequential steps, mimicking human logic and reducing errors. However, complex tasks with vast solution spaces and vague constraints often exceed the capacity of a single reasoning chain. Inspired by Minimal Free Resolution (MFR) in commutative algebra and algebraic geometry, we propose Syzygy of Thoughts (SoT)-a novel framework that extends CoT by introducing auxiliary, interrelated reasoning paths. SoT captures deeper logical dependencies, enabling more robust and structured problem-solving. MFR decomposes a module into a sequence of free modules with minimal rank, providing a structured analytical approach to complex systems. This method introduces the concepts of “Module”, “Betti numbers”,“Freeness”, “Mapping”, “Exactness” and “Minimality”, enabling the systematic decomposition of the original complex problem into logically complete minimal subproblems while preserving key problem features and reducing reasoning length. We tested SoT across diverse datasets (e.g., GSM8K, MATH) and models (e.g., GPT-4o-mini, Qwen2.5), achieving inference accuracy that matches or surpasses mainstream CoTs standards. Additionally, by aligning the sampling process with algebraic constraints, our approach enhances the scalability of inference time in LLMs, ensuring both transparent reasoning and high performance. Our code will be publicly available at this https URL.
zh

[NLP-82] How new data permeates LLM knowledge and how to dilute it

【速读】： 该论文旨在探究大型语言模型（LLMs）在学习新信息时如何影响其现有知识，特别是在有益泛化与有害幻觉之间的权衡机制。论文发现，在学习新信息时，LLMs表现出一种“启动效应”（priming effect），即学习的新事实可能导致模型在无关上下文中不恰当地应用该知识。为系统研究这一现象，作者构建了一个名为“Outlandish”的多样化数据集，用于检测新知识如何渗透到模型的知识库中。通过此数据集，研究揭示了学习新信息后启动效应的程度可以通过测量关键单词的学习前标记概率来预测，并且这种关系在不同架构（如PALM-2、Gemma、Llama）、规模和训练阶段中均保持稳健。

解决方案的关键在于提出两种新颖的技术以调节新知识对现有模型行为的影响：一是“踏脚石”文本增强策略，二是“忽略-k”更新剪枝方法。这些技术能够将不必要的启动效应减少50%-95%，同时保留模型学习新信息的能力。这不仅提供了关于LLMs学习方式的经验性见解，还为提升语言模型知识插入的特异性提供了实用工具。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09522
作者: Chen Sun,Renat Aksitov,Andrey Zhmoginov,Nolan Andrew Miller,Max Vladymyrov,Ulrich Rueckert,Been Kim,Mark Sandler
机构: Google DeepMind
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large language models learn and continually learn through the accumulation of gradient-based updates, but how individual pieces of new information affect existing knowledge, leading to both beneficial generalization and problematic hallucination, remains poorly understood. We demonstrate that when learning new information, LLMs exhibit a “priming” effect: learning a new fact can cause the model to inappropriately apply that knowledge in unrelated contexts. To systematically study this phenomenon, we introduce “Outlandish,” a carefully curated dataset of 1320 diverse text samples designed to probe how new knowledge permeates through an LLM’s existing knowledge base. Using this dataset, we show that the degree of priming after learning new information can be predicted by measuring the token probability of key words before learning. This relationship holds robustly across different model architectures (PALM-2, Gemma, Llama), sizes, and training stages. Finally, we develop two novel techniques to modulate how new knowledge affects existing model behavior: (1) a stepping-stone'' text augmentation strategy and (2) an ignore-k’’ update pruning method. These approaches reduce undesirable priming effects by 50-95% while preserving the model’s ability to learn new information. Our findings provide both empirical insights into how LLMs learn and practical tools for improving the specificity of knowledge insertion in language models. Further materials: this https URL
zh

[NLP-83] MADLLM : Multivariate Anomaly Detection via Pre-trained LLM s ICME2025

【速读】： 该论文试图解决当将预训练的大规模语言模型（Large Language Models, LLMs）应用于异常检测任务时，其文本模态与多变量时间序列（Multivariate Time Series, MTS）模态不匹配的问题。现有方法通常将MTS数据简单转换为多个单变量时间序列，这会导致许多问题。论文提出的关键解决方案是MADLLM，一种通过预训练LLMs实现的新型多变量异常检测方法。其核心在于设计了一种新的三重编码技术，将MTS模态与LLMs的文本模态对齐。具体而言，该技术结合了传统的补丁嵌入方法与两种新颖的嵌入方式：Skip嵌入，通过改变传统方法中补丁处理的顺序，帮助LLMs保留先前特征的知识；Feature嵌入，利用对比学习使模型更好地理解不同特征之间的相关性。实验结果表明，该方法在多种公开异常检测数据集上优于现有最先进方法。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09504
作者: Wei Tao,Xiaoyang Qu,Kai Lu,Jiguang Wan,Guokuan Li,Jianzong Wang
机构: Huazhong University of Science and Technology (华中科技大学); Ping An Technology (Shenzhen) Co., Ltd. (平安科技（深圳）有限公司)
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Accepted by IEEE International Conference on Multimedia Expo 2025 (ICME 2025)

点击查看摘要

Abstract:When applying pre-trained large language models (LLMs) to address anomaly detection tasks, the multivariate time series (MTS) modality of anomaly detection does not align with the text modality of LLMs. Existing methods simply transform the MTS data into multiple univariate time series sequences, which can cause many problems. This paper introduces MADLLM, a novel multivariate anomaly detection method via pre-trained LLMs. We design a new triple encoding technique to align the MTS modality with the text modality of LLMs. Specifically, this technique integrates the traditional patch embedding method with two novel embedding approaches: Skip Embedding, which alters the order of patch processing in traditional methods to help LLMs retain knowledge of previous features, and Feature Embedding, which leverages contrastive learning to allow the model to better understand the correlations between different features. Experimental results demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art methods in various public anomaly detection datasets.
zh

[NLP-84] Kongzi: A Historical Large Language Model with Fact Enhancement

【速读】： 该论文试图解决大型语言模型（Large Language Models, LLMs）在复杂推理任务中，尤其是在历史分析等知识密集型领域中存在的事实准确性不足和推理深度受限的问题。论文指出，当前的推理模型在较长的推理链中常表现出事实性错误，这限制了其在历史推理等任务中的表现。为了解决这些问题，论文提出了一种专门设计用于历史分析的大规模语言模型——Kongzi。其解决方案的关键在于通过整合高质量的历史数据集以及采用一种新颖的事实增强学习策略，使模型不仅能够实现更高的事实一致性，还具备更深层次的推理能力。实验结果表明，Kongzi在历史问答和叙事生成等任务中，显著优于现有模型，在事实准确性与推理深度方面均展现出卓越性能。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09488
作者: Jiashu Yang,Ningning Wang,Yian Zhao,Chaoran Feng,Junjia Du,Hao Pang,Zhirui Fang,Xuxin Cheng
机构: Dalian University of Technology (大连理工大学); Nanyang Technological University (南洋理工大学); Peking University (北京大学)
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 22 pages, 12 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The capabilities of the latest large language models (LLMs) have been extended from pure natural language understanding to complex reasoning tasks. However, current reasoning models often exhibit factual inaccuracies in longer reasoning chains, which poses challenges for historical reasoning and limits the potential of LLMs in complex, knowledge-intensive tasks. Historical studies require not only the accurate presentation of factual information but also the ability to establish cross-temporal correlations and derive coherent conclusions from fragmentary and often ambiguous sources. To address these challenges, we propose Kongzi, a large language model specifically designed for historical analysis. Through the integration of curated, high-quality historical data and a novel fact-reinforcement learning strategy, Kongzi demonstrates strong factual alignment and sophisticated reasoning depth. Extensive experiments on tasks such as historical question answering and narrative generation demonstrate that Kongzi outperforms existing models in both factual accuracy and reasoning depth. By effectively addressing the unique challenges inherent in historical texts, Kongzi sets a new standard for the development of accurate and reliable LLMs in professional domains.
zh

[NLP-85] HalluShift: Measuring Distribution Shifts towards Hallucination Detection in LLM s

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决大型语言模型（Large Language Models, LLMs）在生成响应时因内在动态特性导致的幻觉（hallucinations）问题，即模型倾向于生成看似结构良好且连贯但包含不正确信息的响应。论文的关键假设是幻觉源于LLMs的内部动力学，并观察到这种现象表现为模型在生成段落时逐渐偏离事实准确性，尤其是在响应的细微部分，最终可能导致错误信息的传播。受人类认知中类似现象的启发，研究引入了一种名为HalluShift的新方法，通过分析LLM生成响应中的内部状态空间分布偏移和标记概率变化来深入探究这一问题。这种方法在多个基准数据集上的表现优于现有基线，体现了其有效性。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09482
作者: Sharanya Dasgupta,Sujoy Nath,Arkaprabha Basu,Pourya Shamsolmoali,Swagatam Das
机构: Electronics and Communication Sciences Unit (ECSU), Indian Statistical Institute, Kolkata, India; Netaji Subhash Engineering College (NSEC), Kolkata, India; East China Normal University (ECNU), China
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Emerging Technologies (cs.ET)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently garnered widespread attention due to their adeptness at generating innovative responses to the given prompts across a multitude of domains. However, LLMs often suffer from the inherent limitation of hallucinations and generate incorrect information while maintaining well-structured and coherent responses. In this work, we hypothesize that hallucinations stem from the internal dynamics of LLMs. Our observations indicate that, during passage generation, LLMs tend to deviate from factual accuracy in subtle parts of responses, eventually shifting toward misinformation. This phenomenon bears a resemblance to human cognition, where individuals may hallucinate while maintaining logical coherence, embedding uncertainty within minor segments of their speech. To investigate this further, we introduce an innovative approach, HalluShift, designed to analyze the distribution shifts in the internal state space and token probabilities of the LLM-generated responses. Our method attains superior performance compared to existing baselines across various benchmark datasets. Our codebase is available at this https URL.
zh

[NLP-86] Draw with Thought: Unleashing Multimodal Reasoning for Scientific Diagram Generation

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决科学图表（Scientific Diagrams）因以静态光栅图像形式发表而丢失符号语义（symbolic semantics）且限制重用的问题。现有方法虽利用多模态大语言模型（Multimodal Large Language Models, MLLMs）尝试连接视觉与结构，但缺乏语义控制（semantic control）和结构可解释性（structural interpretability），尤其在复杂图表上表现不足。
解决方案的关键在于提出Draw with Thought (DwT)，一种无需微调（training-free）的框架，通过认知驱动的链式思维推理（cognitively-grounded Chain-of-Thought reasoning）引导MLLMs将图表重构为可编辑的mxGraph XML代码。DwT通过“粗到细规划”（Coarse-to-Fine Planning）和“结构感知代码生成”（Structure-Aware Code Generation）两阶段任务实现这一目标：前者负责感知结构化处理和语义指定，后者通过格式引导优化提升结果质量。此外，论文发布了Plot2XML数据集作为基准，包含247个真实科学图表及其金标准XML标注，验证了该方法在高保真度、语义一致性及结构有效性方面的优越性能。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09479
作者: Zhiqing Cui,Jiahao Yuan,Hanqing Wang,Yanshu Li,Chenxu Du,Zhenglong Ding
机构: Nanjing University of Information Science & Technology (南京信息工程大学); East China Normal University (华东师范大学); Huazhong University of Science and Technology (华中科技大学); Brown University (布朗大学); Southwest Jiaotong University (西南交通大学)
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 26 pages, 14 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Scientific diagrams are vital tools for communicating structured knowledge across disciplines. However, they are often published as static raster images, losing symbolic semantics and limiting reuse. While Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) offer a pathway to bridging vision and structure, existing methods lack semantic control and structural interpretability, especially on complex diagrams. We propose Draw with Thought (DwT), a training-free framework that guides MLLMs to reconstruct diagrams into editable mxGraph XML code through cognitively-grounded Chain-of-Thought reasoning. DwT enables interpretable and controllable outputs without model fine-tuning by dividing the task into two stages: Coarse-to-Fine Planning, which handles perceptual structuring and semantic specification, and Structure-Aware Code Generation, enhanced by format-guided refinement. To support evaluation, we release Plot2XML, a benchmark of 247 real-world scientific diagrams with gold-standard XML annotations. Extensive experiments across eight MLLMs show that our approach yields high-fidelity, semantically aligned, and structurally valid reconstructions, with human evaluations confirming strong alignment in both accuracy and visual aesthetics, offering a scalable solution for converting static visuals into executable representations and advancing machine understanding of scientific graphics.
zh

[NLP-87] AdaSteer: Your Aligned LLM is Inherently an Adaptive Jailbreak Defender

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决大型语言模型（Large Language Models, LLMs）在安全对齐方面仍易受越狱攻击（jailbreak attacks）的问题。现有的无训练防御方法如激活引导（activation steering）依赖固定的引导系数，这导致保护效果次优且对良性输入的误拒率增加。为了解决这一问题，论文提出了一种自适应激活引导方法AdaSteer。其关键是通过识别两个关键特性：拒绝定律（Rejection Law, R-Law），即需要对反向拒绝方向的越狱输入施加更强的引导；有害性定律（Harmfulness Law, H-Law），即区分对抗性和良性输入。AdaSteer通过逻辑回归学习自适应系数，在拒绝方向（Rejection Direction, RD）和有害性方向（Harmfulness Direction, HD）上引导输入表示，从而实现对越狱攻击的稳健防御，同时保持对良性输入的良好处理能力。实验结果表明，AdaSteer在多个越狱攻击场景下优于基线方法，并对模型实用性影响较小。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09466
作者: Weixiang Zhao,Jiahe Guo,Yulin Hu,Yang Deng,An Zhang,Xingyu Sui,Xinyang Han,Yanyan Zhao,Bing Qin,Tat-Seng Chua,Ting Liu
机构: Harbin Institute of Technology (哈尔滨工业大学); Singapore Management University (新加坡管理大学); National University of Singapore (新加坡国立大学)
类目: Cryptography and Security (cs.CR); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 17 pages, 6 figures, 9 tables

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Despite extensive efforts in safety alignment, large language models (LLMs) remain vulnerable to jailbreak attacks. Activation steering offers a training-free defense method but relies on fixed steering coefficients, resulting in suboptimal protection and increased false rejections of benign inputs. To address this, we propose AdaSteer, an adaptive activation steering method that dynamically adjusts model behavior based on input characteristics. We identify two key properties: Rejection Law (R-Law), which shows that stronger steering is needed for jailbreak inputs opposing the rejection direction, and Harmfulness Law (H-Law), which differentiates adversarial and benign inputs. AdaSteer steers input representations along both the Rejection Direction (RD) and Harmfulness Direction (HD), with adaptive coefficients learned via logistic regression, ensuring robust jailbreak defense while preserving benign input handling. Experiments on LLaMA-3.1, Gemma-2, and Qwen2.5 show that AdaSteer outperforms baseline methods across multiple jailbreak attacks with minimal impact on utility. Our results highlight the potential of interpretable model internals for real-time, flexible safety enforcement in LLMs.
zh

[NLP-88] BabyVLM: Data-Efficient Pretraining of VLMs Inspired by Infant Learning

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决现有视觉语言模型（Vision-Language Models, VLMs）预训练效率低下以及现有评估基准与婴儿视觉推理发展不一致的问题。具体而言，现有的婴儿启发式数据集如SAYCam虽被采用，但当前评估基准要么过于简单化，要么范围狭窄，或专门为大规模预训练模型设计，且单纯依赖婴儿数据忽略了婴儿从更广泛、更多样化输入中学习的特性。为了解决这些问题，论文提出BabyVLM框架，其关键是构建了一个综合的领域内评估基准以及通过现有数据集的儿童导向变换生成的合成训练数据集。实验表明，使用该合成数据集训练的VLM在BabyVLM任务上的表现优于仅基于SAYCam或同等规模通用数据训练的模型，从而证明了精心策划的小型数据集可以有效提升模型的泛化能力，并为高效的数据驱动视觉语言学习范式开辟了道路。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09426
作者: Shengao Wang,Arjun Chandra,Aoming Liu,Venkatesh Saligrama,Boqing Gong
机构: Boston University (波士顿大学); Boston University (波士顿大学); Boston University (波士顿大学); Boston University (波士顿大学); Boston University (波士顿大学)
类目: Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Human infants rapidly develop visual reasoning skills from minimal input, suggesting that developmentally inspired pretraining could significantly enhance the efficiency of vision-language models (VLMs). Although recent efforts have leveraged infant-inspired datasets like SAYCam, existing evaluation benchmarks remain misaligned–they are either too simplistic, narrowly scoped, or tailored for large-scale pretrained models. Additionally, training exclusively on infant data overlooks the broader, diverse input from which infants naturally learn. To address these limitations, we propose BabyVLM, a novel framework comprising comprehensive in-domain evaluation benchmarks and a synthetic training dataset created via child-directed transformations of existing datasets. We demonstrate that VLMs trained with our synthetic dataset achieve superior performance on BabyVLM tasks compared to models trained solely on SAYCam or general-purpose data of the SAYCam size. BabyVLM thus provides a robust, developmentally aligned evaluation tool and illustrates how compact models trained on carefully curated data can generalize effectively, opening pathways toward data-efficient vision-language learning paradigms.
zh

[NLP-89] ClinicalGPT -R1: Pushing reasoning capability of generalist disease diagnosis with large language model

【速读】： 该论文试图解决大型语言模型（Large Language Models, LLMs）在临床诊断领域应用不足的问题。解决方案的关键在于开发ClinicalGPT-R1，这是一种针对疾病诊断增强推理能力的通用型大语言模型。通过在包含20,000份真实世界临床记录的数据集上进行训练，并采用多样化的训练策略以提升诊断推理能力，ClinicalGPT-R1实现了在中文诊断任务中超越GPT-4o的表现，并在英文环境下与GPT-4达到相当的性能水平。这一比较研究有效验证了ClinicalGPT-R1在疾病诊断任务中的卓越表现。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09421
作者: Wuyang Lan,Wenzheng Wang,Changwei Ji,Guoxing Yang,Yongbo Zhang,Xiaohong Liu,Song Wu,Guangyu Wang
机构: State Key Laboratory of Networking and Switching Technology (网络与交换技术国家重点实验室) Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications (北京邮电大学); South China Hospital, Medical School, Shenzhen University (深圳大学华南医院)
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注: 8 pages, 6 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Recent advances in reasoning with large language models (LLMs)has shown remarkable reasoning capabilities in domains such as mathematics and coding, yet their application to clinical diagnosis remains underexplored. Here, we introduce ClinicalGPT-R1, a reasoning enhanced generalist large language model for disease diagnosis. Trained on a dataset of 20,000 real-world clinical records, ClinicalGPT-R1 leverages diverse training strategies to enhance diagnostic reasoning. To benchmark performance, we curated MedBench-Hard, a challenging dataset spanning seven major medical specialties and representative diseases. Experimental results demonstrate that ClinicalGPT-R1 outperforms GPT-4o in Chinese diagnostic tasks and achieves comparable performance to GPT-4 in English settings. This comparative study effectively validates the superior performance of ClinicalGPT-R1 in disease diagnosis tasks. Resources are available at this https URL.
zh

[NLP-90] SaRO: Enhancing LLM Safety through Reasoning -based Alignment

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决大型语言模型（Large Language Models, LLMs）安全对齐中的两个关键问题：(1) 欠泛化导致模型易受新型越狱攻击（jailbreak attacks）的影响，以及 (2) 过度对齐引发对良性指令的过度拒绝。研究发现嵌入空间中越狱/有害查询与正常提示之间存在语义重叠，表明需要更深层次的语义理解以实现更有效的安全性对齐。为此，论文提出了一种以安全策略驱动推理的安全导向推理优化框架（Safety-oriented Reasoning Optimization Framework, SaRO），其关键是通过两阶段方法实现：(1) 推理风格预热（Reasoning-style Warmup, RW），利用有监督微调使LLMs内化长链推理能力；(2) 安全导向推理过程优化（Safety-oriented Reasoning Process Optimization, SRPO），通过直接偏好优化（Direct Preference Optimization, DPO）促进安全性反思。实验结果验证了SaRO相较于传统对齐方法的优越性。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09420
作者: Yutao Mou,Yuxiao Luo,Shikun Zhang,Wei Ye
机构: National Engineering Research Center for Software Engineering, Peking University (北京大学工程研究中心, 北京大学)
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Current safety alignment techniques for large language models (LLMs) face two key challenges: (1) under-generalization, which leaves models vulnerable to novel jailbreak attacks, and (2) over-alignment, which leads to the excessive refusal of benign instructions. Our preliminary investigation reveals semantic overlap between jailbreak/harmful queries and normal prompts in embedding space, suggesting that more effective safety alignment requires a deeper semantic understanding. This motivates us to incorporate safety-policy-driven reasoning into the alignment process. To this end, we propose the Safety-oriented Reasoning Optimization Framework (SaRO), which consists of two stages: (1) Reasoning-style Warmup (RW) that enables LLMs to internalize long-chain reasoning through supervised fine-tuning, and (2) Safety-oriented Reasoning Process Optimization (SRPO) that promotes safety reflection via direct preference optimization (DPO). Extensive experiments demonstrate the superiority of SaRO over traditional alignment methods.
zh

[NLP-91] UXAgent : A System for Simulating Usability Testing of Web Design with LLM Agents

【速读】： 该论文试图解决如何评估和迭代可用性测试研究设计本身的问题。解决方案的关键在于设计了一个名为 UXAgent 的系统，该系统利用大型语言模型模拟代理（Large Language Model-simulated Agent, LLM Agent）技术，通过其 Persona Generator 模块、LLM Agent 模块以及 Universal Browser Connector 模块自动生成数千个模拟用户来交互式测试目标网站。此外，系统提供了 Agent Interview Interface 和 Video Replay Interface，使用户体验（UX）研究人员能够便捷地审查和分析生成的定性和定量日志数据，从而支持 UX 研究人员在真实的人类主体研究之前对可用性测试研究设计进行评估和迭代。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09407
作者: Yuxuan Lu,Bingsheng Yao,Hansu Gu,Jing Huang,Jessie Wang,Yang Li,Jiri Gesi,Qi He,Toby Jia-Jun Li,Dakuo Wang
机构: Northeastern University (东北大学); University of Notre Dame (圣母大学); Amazon (亚马逊)
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Human-Computer Interaction (cs.HC)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Usability testing is a fundamental research method that user experience (UX) researchers use to evaluate and iterate a web design, but\textbf how to evaluate and iterate the usability testing study design itself? Recent advances in Large Language Model-simulated Agent (\textbfLLM Agent) research inspired us to design \textbfUXAgent to support UX researchers in evaluating and reiterating their usability testing study design before they conduct the real human-subject study. Our system features a Persona Generator module, an LLM Agent module, and a Universal Browser Connector module to automatically generate thousands of simulated users to interactively test the target website. The system also provides an Agent Interview Interface and a Video Replay Interface so that the UX researchers can easily review and analyze the generated qualitative and quantitative log data. Through a heuristic evaluation, five UX researcher participants praised the innovation of our system but also expressed concerns about the future of LLM Agent usage in UX studies.
zh

[NLP-92] Question Tokens Deserve More Attention: Enhancing Large Language Models without Training through Step-by-Step Reading and Question Attention Recalibration

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决大型语言模型（Large Language Models, LLMs）在处理需要深入理解复杂问题的任务时所面临的挑战，特别是在涉及长距离依赖或多步推理的情况下。论文通过研究当前LLMs在问题理解方面的局限性，提出了三个关键洞察：(1) 重复问题标记可以提高对问题区域的关注从而增强理解；(2) 增加向后的依赖关系会因单向注意力限制而影响性能；(3) 重新校准注意力机制以优先关注与问题相关区域能够提升表现。基于这些发现，论文首先提出了一系列基于提示的方法——逐步阅读（Step-by-Step Reading, SSR）、SSR+ 和 SSR++，引导LLMs逐步处理问题标记并将推理过程与输入结构对齐，显著提升了性能，在多个基准测试中达到了最先进的结果。其次，引入了一种无需训练的注意力重新校准机制，在推理过程中动态调整注意力分布以强调与问题相关的区域。此方法在不改变模型参数或输入提示的情况下，将LLaMA 3.1-8B在AQuA上的准确性提高了5.17%。综合来看，研究结果强调了结构化提示设计和注意力优化在改善LLMs理解能力中的重要性，提供了轻量但有效的工具来提升自然语言处理任务的表现。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09402
作者: Feijiang Han,Licheng Guo,Hengtao Cui,Zhiyuan Lyu
机构: University of Pennsylvania (宾夕法尼亚大学)
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注: CIS 5300

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large Language Models (LLMs) often struggle with tasks that require a deep understanding of complex questions, especially when faced with long-range dependencies or multi-step reasoning. This work investigates the limitations of current LLMs in question comprehension and identifies three insights: (1) repeating question tokens improves comprehension by increasing attention to question regions, (2) increased backward dependencies negatively affect performance due to unidirectional attentional constraints, and (3) recalibrating attentional mechanisms to prioritize question-relevant regions improves performance. Based on these findings, we first propose a family of prompt-based strategies - Step-by-Step Reading (SSR), SSR+, and SSR++ - that guide LLMs to incrementally process question tokens and align their reasoning with the input structure. These methods significantly improve performance, with SSR++ achieving state-of-the-art results on several benchmarks: 96.66% on GSM8K, 94.61% on ASDiv, and 76.28% on AQuA. Second, we introduce a training-free attention recalibration mechanism that dynamically adjusts attention distributions during inference to emphasize question-relevant regions. This method improves the accuracy of LLaMA 3.1-8B on AQuA by 5.17% without changing model parameters or input prompts. Taken together, our results highlight the importance of structured prompt design and attention optimization in improving LLM comprehension, providing lightweight yet effective tools for improving performance in various NLP tasks. Comments: CIS 5300 Subjects: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI) Cite as: arXiv:2504.09402 [cs.CL] (or arXiv:2504.09402v1 [cs.CL] for this version) https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2504.09402 Focus to learn more arXiv-issued DOI via DataCite (pending registration)
zh

[NLP-93] Composable NLP Workflows for BERT-based Ranking and QA System

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决多任务交互处理中的难题，特别是在不同粒度文本上处理跨任务交互的繁琐性。为应对这一挑战，论文提出的关键解决方案是构建一个端到端的排名（Ranking）与问答（QA）系统，采用Forte工具包实现可组合的自然语言处理（NLP）流水线。该系统利用了如BERT和RoBERTa等最先进的深度学习模型，并通过MS-MARCO和Covid-19数据集评估性能，使用BLUE、MRR、F1等指标与相应基准结果进行对比。其关键之处在于流水线的模块化设计以及重排序器（reranker）的低延迟特性，这使得复杂NLP应用的构建变得简便高效。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09398
作者: Gaurav Kumar,Murali Mohana Krishna Dandu
机构: UC San Diego (加州大学圣地亚哥分校); UC San Diego (加州大学圣地亚哥分校)
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注: 6 pages, 3 figures, 6 tables

点击查看摘要

Abstract:There has been a lot of progress towards building NLP models that scale to multiple tasks. However, real-world systems contain multiple components and it is tedious to handle cross-task interaction with varying levels of text granularity. In this work, we built an end-to-end Ranking and Question-Answering (QA) system using Forte, a toolkit that makes composable NLP pipelines. We utilized state-of-the-art deep learning models such as BERT, RoBERTa in our pipeline, evaluated the performance on MS-MARCO and Covid-19 datasets using metrics such as BLUE, MRR, F1 and compared the results of ranking and QA systems with their corresponding benchmark results. The modular nature of our pipeline and low latency of reranker makes it easy to build complex NLP applications easily.
zh

[NLP-94] Evaluation Under Imperfect Benchmarks and Ratings: A Case Study in Text Simplification

【速读】： 该论文试图解决文本简化任务中现有评估方法不可靠的问题，主要面临两个挑战：一是现有基准数据可能无法充分反映当前语言模型在该任务上的能力，包含不流畅、不连贯或过于简单的例子；二是基准数据的人类评分存在高度分歧，导致评价结果不一致。尽管已有指标努力与这些不完美的评分保持较高相关性，但评估结果仍缺乏可靠性和预期趋势（如更强大的模型应获得更高的分数）。

解决方案的关键在于通过三个贡献优化评估过程：首先，提出SynthSimpliEval，一个基于不同规模模型生成简化句子的合成基准，证明其上的人类评分具有高一致性且符合预期趋势；其次，验证使用大型语言模型（LLMs）作为评审小组进行自动评估通常足以获得一致的评分；最后，展示现有的可学习评估指标通过在LLMs-as-a-jury标注的合成数据上训练后，能够缩小与纯LLMs-as-a-jury评估之间的差距。总体而言，通过文本简化案例研究，论文表明可靠的评估需要更高质量的测试数据，可通过合成数据和LLMs-as-a-jury评分获得。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09394
作者: Joseph Liu,Yoonsoo Nam,Xinyue Cui,Swabha Swayamdipta
机构: University of Southern California (南加州大学)
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Submitted to COLM 2025. 9 pages, 6 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Despite the successes of language models, their evaluation remains a daunting challenge for new and existing tasks. We consider the task of text simplification, commonly used to improve information accessibility, where evaluation faces two major challenges. First, the data in existing benchmarks might not reflect the capabilities of current language models on the task, often containing disfluent, incoherent, or simplistic examples. Second, existing human ratings associated with the benchmarks often contain a high degree of disagreement, resulting in inconsistent ratings; nevertheless, existing metrics still have to show higher correlations with these imperfect ratings. As a result, evaluation for the task is not reliable and does not reflect expected trends (e.g., more powerful models being assigned higher scores). We address these challenges for the task of text simplification through three contributions. First, we introduce SynthSimpliEval, a synthetic benchmark for text simplification featuring simplified sentences generated by models of varying sizes. Through a pilot study, we show that human ratings on our benchmark exhibit high inter-annotator agreement and reflect the expected trend: larger models produce higher-quality simplifications. Second, we show that auto-evaluation with a panel of LLM judges (LLMs-as-a-jury) often suffices to obtain consistent ratings for the evaluation of text simplification. Third, we demonstrate that existing learnable metrics for text simplification benefit from training on our LLMs-as-a-jury-rated synthetic data, closing the gap with pure LLMs-as-a-jury for evaluation. Overall, through our case study on text simplification, we show that a reliable evaluation requires higher quality test data, which could be obtained through synthetic data and LLMs-as-a-jury ratings.
zh

[NLP-95] Beyond Memorization: Mapping the Originality-Quality Frontier of Language Models

【速读】： 该论文试图解决大型语言模型（Large Language Models, LLMs）在创意任务中生成新颖输出的能力评估问题。传统方法仅从训练数据的角度衡量新颖性，忽略了生成质量；而基于人类偏好的评估又可能倾向于高质量但缺乏原创性的内容，导致评估可靠性不足。为此，论文提出了一种新的新颖性度量方法，即通过训练过程中未见的n-gram比例与特定任务的质量评分的调和平均值来平衡原创性和质量。解决方案的关键在于引入这一综合考虑原创性和质量的新颖性度量，并通过实验验证不同方法对提升生成文本新颖性的影响，同时揭示了原创性与质量之间的权衡关系。研究表明，增加模型规模或应用后训练技术能够更有效地提升新颖性，而非单纯依赖推理阶段的调整方法。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09389
作者: Vishakh Padmakumar,Chen Yueh-Han,Jane Pan,Valerie Chen,He He
机构: New York University; Carnegie Mellon University (卡内基梅隆大学)
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for ideation and scientific discovery, it is important to evaluate their ability to generate novel output. Prior work evaluates novelty as the originality with respect to training data, but original outputs can be low quality. In contrast, non-expert judges may favor high-quality but memorized outputs, limiting the reliability of human preference as a metric. We propose a new novelty metric for LLM generations that balances originality and quality – the harmonic mean of the fraction of \ngrams unseen during training and a task-specific quality score. We evaluate the novelty of generations from two families of open-data models (OLMo and Pythia) on three creative tasks: story completion, poetry writing, and creative tool use. We find that LLM generated text is less novel than human written text. To elicit more novel outputs, we experiment with various inference-time methods, which reveals a trade-off between originality and quality. While these methods can boost novelty, they do so by increasing originality at the expense of quality. In contrast, increasing model size or applying post-training reliably shifts the Pareto frontier, highlighting that starting with a stronger base model is a more effective way to improve novelty.
zh

[NLP-96] On Language Models Sensitivity to Suspicious Coincidences

【速读】： 该论文试图解决的问题是：分析语言模型（Language Models, LMs）是否表现出与人类相似的“可疑巧合”（suspicious coincidence）效应，即在归纳推理过程中，倾向于选择更小、更具体的假设而非更通用的假设。这一现象反映了人类对数据采样方式的敏感性以及对任务沟通目标的理解。

解决方案的关键在于通过两种实验设置来研究语言模型的行为：1）数字游戏任务，评估模型对一组数字是否适合某个候选数字的判断；2）扩展城市名称的游戏场景。研究者发现，在零样本（zero-shot）条件下，语言模型并未显示出明显的“可疑巧合”效应。然而，当通过链式思维（chain-of-thought）提示或显式提供假设空间后，模型开始表现出类似人类的“可疑巧合”效应。这表明，语言模型的归纳推理能力可以通过显式访问假设空间得以增强。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09387
作者: Sriram Padmanabhan,Kanishka Misra,Kyle Mahowald,Eunsol Choi
机构: The University of Texas at Austin (德克萨斯大学奥斯汀分校); Toyota Technological Institute at Chicago (芝加哥丰田技术研究所); New York University (纽约大学)
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Humans are sensitive to suspicious coincidences when generalizing inductively over data, as they make assumptions as to how the data was sampled. This results in smaller, more specific hypotheses being favored over more general ones. For instance, when provided the set Austin, Dallas, Houston, one is more likely to think that this is sampled from “Texas Cities” over “US Cities” even though both are compatible. Suspicious coincidence is strongly connected to pragmatic reasoning, and can serve as a testbed to analyze systems on their sensitivity towards the communicative goals of the task (i.e., figuring out the true category underlying the data). In this paper, we analyze whether suspicious coincidence effects are reflected in language models’ (LMs) behavior. We do so in the context of two domains: 1) the number game, where humans made judgments of whether a number (e.g., 4) fits a list of given numbers (e.g., 16, 32, 2); and 2) by extending the number game setup to prominent cities. For both domains, the data is compatible with multiple hypotheses and we study which hypothesis is most consistent with the models’ behavior. On analyzing five models, we do not find strong evidence for suspicious coincidences in LMs’ zero-shot behavior. However, when provided access to the hypotheses space via chain-of-thought or explicit prompting, LMs start to show an effect resembling suspicious coincidences, sometimes even showing effects consistent with humans. Our study suggests that inductive reasoning behavior in LMs can be enhanced with explicit access to the hypothesis landscape.
zh

[NLP-97] Can you map it to English? The Role of Cross-Lingual Alignment in Multilingual Performance of LLM s

【速读】： 该论文试图解决大型语言模型（Large Language Models, LLMs）在多语言文本表示对齐机制及其与自然语言理解及翻译任务性能关联性的理解不足这一问题。论文的关键解决方案在于引入跨语言对齐度量指标，如辨别性对齐指数（Discriminative Alignment Index, DALI），用于量化实例级别的对齐情况，并通过在三种自然语言理解任务（Belebele、XStoryCloze、XCOPA）以及机器翻译任务上的实验，揭示了跨语言对齐在语言级别上与任务准确性有强相关性，但在样本级别上往往无法有效区分正确与错误预测，表明对齐是成功的必要但不充分条件。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09378
作者: Kartik Ravisankar,Hyojung Han,Marine Carpuat
机构: University of Maryland (马里兰大学)
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large language models (LLMs) pre-trained predominantly on English text exhibit surprising multilingual capabilities, yet the mechanisms driving cross-lingual generalization remain poorly understood. This work investigates how the alignment of representations for text written in different languages correlates with LLM performance on natural language understanding tasks and translation tasks, both at the language and the instance level. For this purpose, we introduce cross-lingual alignment metrics such as the Discriminative Alignment Index (DALI) to quantify the alignment at an instance level for discriminative tasks. Through experiments on three natural language understanding tasks (Belebele, XStoryCloze, XCOPA), and machine translation, we find that while cross-lingual alignment metrics strongly correlate with task accuracy at the language level, the sample-level alignment often fails to distinguish correct from incorrect predictions, exposing alignment as a necessary but insufficient condition for success.
zh

[NLP-98] QUDsim: Quantifying Discourse Similarities in LLM -Generated Text

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决大型语言模型（Large Language Models, LLMs）在生成独特且富有创意内容方面的不足问题。尽管LLMs能够覆盖多样主题生成文本，但普遍存在重复性高的现象。现有基于词项重叠和句法模式的相似性度量主要捕捉内容层面的重叠，无法有效检测话语结构上的相似性。为解决此问题，论文的关键在于引入基于问题-under-讨论（Questions Under Discussion, QUD）及其语义理论的话语抽象方法，以量化不同文本间话语进展的差异，并据此构建了一个名为QUDsim的新相似性度量工具。通过QUDsim，研究发现LLMs在跨样本生成时频繁复用话语结构（比人类更甚），不仅表现出结构性单一性和重复性，还与人类作者使用的话语结构类型存在偏差。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09373
作者: Ramya Namuduri,Yating Wu,Anshun Asher Zheng,Manya Wadhwa,Greg Durrett,Junyi Jessy Li
机构: 未知
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:As large language models become increasingly capable at various writing tasks, their weakness at generating unique and creative content becomes a major liability. Although LLMs have the ability to generate text covering diverse topics, there is an overall sense of repetitiveness across texts that we aim to formalize and quantify via a similarity metric. The familiarity between documents arises from the persistence of underlying discourse structures. However, existing similarity metrics dependent on lexical overlap and syntactic patterns largely capture \textitcontent overlap, thus making them unsuitable for detecting \textitstructural similarities. We introduce an abstraction based on linguistic theories in Questions Under Discussion (QUD) and question semantics to help quantify differences in discourse progression. We then use this framework to build \textbfQUDsim , a similarity metric that can detect discursive parallels between documents. Using QUDsim, we find that LLMs often reuse discourse structures (more so than humans) across samples, even when content differs. Furthermore, LLMs are not only repetitive and structurally uniform, but are also divergent from human authors in the types of structures they use.
zh

[NLP-99] REMEMBER: Retrieval-based Explainable Multimodal Evidence-guided Modeling for Brain Evaluation and Reasoning in Zero- and Few-shot Neurodegenerative Diagnosis

【速读】： 本文旨在解决神经退行性疾病（如阿尔茨海默病）诊断中现有深度学习模型依赖大规模标注数据且缺乏可解释性的问题。特别是在临床实践中，数据通常规模较小或未标注，限制了深度学习方法的潜力。为应对这些挑战，研究引入了REMEMBER框架，其核心在于通过基于参考的推理过程实现零样本和少样本阿尔茨海默病诊断。关键解决方案是首先利用专家注释的数据训练对比对齐的视觉-文本模型，并扩展伪文本模态以编码异常类型、诊断标签及复合临床描述；在推理阶段，从精心策划的数据集中检索相似的人类验证案例，并通过专用的证据编码模块和注意力机制推理头整合上下文信息，从而实现基于证据的预测，生成可解释的诊断报告，包括参考图像与临床工作流程一致的解释。这种设计使REMEMBER能够模仿真实的临床决策过程，同时在有限数据条件下展现出强大的实际应用价值。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09354
作者: Duy-Cat Can,Quang-Huy Tang,Huong Ha,Binh T. Nguyen,Oliver Y. Chén
机构: Lausanne University Hospital (CHUV); University of Lausanne (UNIL); Vietnam National University; Department of Computer Science, University of Science, Vietnam National University Ho Chi Minh City; School of Biomedical Engineering, International University, Vietnam National University Ho Chi Minh City; University of Science, Vietnam National University Ho Chi Minh City; Lausanne University Hospital (CHUV); University of Lausanne (UNIL)
类目: Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG); Quantitative Methods (q-bio.QM)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Timely and accurate diagnosis of neurodegenerative disorders, such as Alzheimer’s disease, is central to disease management. Existing deep learning models require large-scale annotated datasets and often function as “black boxes”. Additionally, datasets in clinical practice are frequently small or unlabeled, restricting the full potential of deep learning methods. Here, we introduce REMEMBER – Retrieval-based Explainable Multimodal Evidence-guided Modeling for Brain Evaluation and Reasoning – a new machine learning framework that facilitates zero- and few-shot Alzheimer’s diagnosis using brain MRI scans through a reference-based reasoning process. Specifically, REMEMBER first trains a contrastively aligned vision-text model using expert-annotated reference data and extends pseudo-text modalities that encode abnormality types, diagnosis labels, and composite clinical descriptions. Then, at inference time, REMEMBER retrieves similar, human-validated cases from a curated dataset and integrates their contextual information through a dedicated evidence encoding module and attention-based inference head. Such an evidence-guided design enables REMEMBER to imitate real-world clinical decision-making process by grounding predictions in retrieved imaging and textual context. Specifically, REMEMBER outputs diagnostic predictions alongside an interpretable report, including reference images and explanations aligned with clinical workflows. Experimental results demonstrate that REMEMBER achieves robust zero- and few-shot performance and offers a powerful and explainable framework to neuroimaging-based diagnosis in the real world, especially under limited data.
zh

[NLP-100] Improving the Accuracy and Efficiency of Legal Document Tagging with Large Language Models and Instruction Prompts

【速读】： 该论文致力于解决法律多标签分类任务中面临的挑战，包括法律语言的复杂性、标签间的复杂依赖关系以及显著的标签不平衡问题。为应对这些挑战，论文提出了一种名为Legal-LLM的新方法，其关键是通过微调充分利用大规模语言模型（Large Language Models, LLMs）的指令跟随能力。该方法将多标签分类任务重新定义为结构化生成问题，指导LLM直接输出给定文档的相关法律类别。实验结果表明，Legal-LLM在POSTURE50K和EURLEX57K两个基准数据集上的表现优于多种强基线模型，并通过消融研究和人工评估验证了其在处理标签不平衡及生成相关且准确的法律标签方面的有效性。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09309
作者: Emily Johnson,Xavier Holt,Noah Wilson
机构: University of Massachusetts, Amherst (马萨诸塞大学阿默斯特分校)
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Legal multi-label classification is a critical task for organizing and accessing the vast amount of legal documentation. Despite its importance, it faces challenges such as the complexity of legal language, intricate label dependencies, and significant label imbalance. In this paper, we propose Legal-LLM, a novel approach that leverages the instruction-following capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) through fine-tuning. We reframe the multi-label classification task as a structured generation problem, instructing the LLM to directly output the relevant legal categories for a given document. We evaluate our method on two benchmark datasets, POSTURE50K and EURLEX57K, using micro-F1 and macro-F1 scores. Our experimental results demonstrate that Legal-LLM outperforms a range of strong baseline models, including traditional methods and other Transformer-based approaches. Furthermore, ablation studies and human evaluations validate the effectiveness of our approach, particularly in handling label imbalance and generating relevant and accurate legal labels.
zh

[NLP-101] Enhancing Contrastive Demonstration Selection with Semantic Diversity for Robust In-Context Machine Translation

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决In-Context Learning (ICL) 中演示示例选择对性能高度敏感的问题，特别是现有方法在选择示例时往往忽视了多样性的重要性。论文的关键创新在于提出了一种名为DiverseConE (Diversity-Enhanced Contrastive Example Selection) 的新方法，该方法在对比选择的基础上引入了一个基于嵌入空间不相似性的多样性增强步骤。通过这种方式，DiverseConE 在保持示例间对比性的同时，显著提升了示例集的多样性，从而有效改善了机器翻译任务中的翻译质量。实验结果表明，DiverseConE 在多个语言对（英语-中文、中文-英语、俄语-德语、德语-俄语）的 1-shot 和 3-shot 设置下均优于随机选择、BM25、TopK 等基线方法以及最先进的对比选择方法，并通过多样性和人工评估进一步验证了其有效性。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09305
作者: Owen Patterson,Chee Ng
机构: Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (马来西亚科技大学)
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In-Context Learning (ICL) empowers large language models to perform tasks by conditioning on a few input-output examples. However, the performance of ICL is highly sensitive to the selection of these demonstrations. While existing methods focus on similarity or contrastive selection, they often overlook the importance of diversity among the chosen examples. In this paper, we propose DiverseConE (Diversity-Enhanced Contrastive Example Selection), a novel approach for demonstration selection in in-context learning for machine translation. Our method builds upon contrastive selection by incorporating a diversity enhancement step based on embedding space dissimilarity. We conduct extensive experiments on the Llama2-7b model across four language pairs (English-Chinese, Chinese-English, Russian-German, German-Russian) in 1-shot and 3-shot settings, using COMET20 and COMET22 for evaluation. Our results demonstrate that DiverseConE consistently outperforms strong baseline methods, including random selection, BM25, TopK, and a state-of-the-art contrastive selection method. Further analysis, including diversity metrics and human evaluation, validates the effectiveness of our approach and highlights the benefits of considering demonstration diversity for improved translation quality.
zh

[NLP-102] Linguistic Comparison of AI- and Human-Written Responses to Online Mental Health Queries

【速读】： 本文旨在探讨生成式人工智能（GenAI）在模拟人类同侪支持方面的能力与局限性，特别是在在线心理健康社区（OMHCs）中提供即时、个性化心理支持的潜力。研究的关键在于评估当前最先进的大型语言模型（LLMs），如GPT-4-Turbo、Llama-3和Mistral-7B，在处理真实世界心理健康相关文本时的表现，并将其输出与人类编写的回应进行对比分析。通过定量的语言学指标以及定性的洞察，研究揭示了AI响应的特点，包括其更高的可读性和结构化表达，但也指出缺乏人际互动中的语言多样性和个人叙述特性。因此，本研究强调了如何在保持AI技术优势的同时，平衡其规模扩展性和时效性与人类交互的真实感、社交性和专业性之间的关系，为未来将GenAI整合进OMHCs提供了伦理与实践上的指导框架。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09271
作者: Koustuv Saha,Yoshee Jain,Munmun De Choudhury
机构: Siebel School of Computing and Data Science, The Grainger College of Engineering, University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign (西贝尔计算与数据科学学院，格兰杰工程学院，伊利诺伊大学香槟分校); School of Interactive Computing, College of Computing, Georgia Institute of Technology (交互计算学院，计算学院，乔治亚理工学院)
类目: Human-Computer Interaction (cs.HC); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Social and Information Networks (cs.SI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The ubiquity and widespread use of digital and online technologies have transformed mental health support, with online mental health communities (OMHCs) providing safe spaces for peer support. More recently, generative AI and large language models (LLMs) have introduced new possibilities for scalable, around-the-clock mental health assistance that could potentially augment and supplement the capabilities of OMHCs. Although genAI shows promise in delivering immediate and personalized responses, their effectiveness in replicating the nuanced, experience-based support of human peers remains an open question. In this study, we harnessed 24,114 posts and 138,758 online community (OC) responses from 55 OMHCs on Reddit. We prompted several state-of-the-art LLMs (GPT-4-Turbo, Llama-3, and Mistral-7B) with these posts, and compared their (AI) responses to human-written (OC) responses based on a variety of linguistic measures across psycholinguistics and lexico-semantics. Our findings revealed that AI responses are more verbose, readable, and analytically structured, but lack linguistic diversity and personal narratives inherent in human-human interactions. Through a qualitative examination, we found validation as well as complementary insights into the nature of AI responses, such as its neutrality of stance and the absence of seeking back-and-forth clarifications. We discuss the ethical and practical implications of integrating generative AI into OMHCs, advocating for frameworks that balance AI’s scalability and timeliness with the irreplaceable authenticity, social interactiveness, and expertise of human connections that form the ethos of online support communities.
zh

[NLP-103] Mixture of Group Experts for Learning Invariant Representations

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决传统稀疏激活Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) 模型中专家多样性与专业化受限的问题，特别是在专家数量增加时性能和可扩展性受到制约的情况。论文提出了一种基于分组稀疏正则化的新方法——Mixture of Group Experts (MoGE)，其关键在于通过在top-k路由输入上施加结构化约束来间接正则化专家，同时保持原始MoE架构不变。此外，MoGE将路由输入组织成二维拓扑图，以空间方式对相邻元素进行分组，从而捕获对小变换不变的表示，显著提升专家的多样性和专业化水平。实验结果表明，MoGE在多种Transformer模型上的图像分类和语言建模任务中明显优于标准MoE，且仅带来极小的额外内存和计算开销。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09265
作者: Lei Kang,Jia Li,Mi Tian,Hua Huang
机构: Beijing Normal University (北京师范大学); TAL Education Group (学而思教育集团)
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Sparsely activated Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models effectively increase the number of parameters while maintaining consistent computational costs per token. However, vanilla MoE models often suffer from limited diversity and specialization among experts, constraining their performance and scalability, especially as the number of experts increases. In this paper, we present a novel perspective on vanilla MoE with top- k routing inspired by sparse representation. This allows us to bridge established theoretical insights from sparse representation into MoE models. Building on this foundation, we propose a group sparse regularization approach for the input of top- k routing, termed Mixture of Group Experts (MoGE). MoGE indirectly regularizes experts by imposing structural constraints on the routing inputs, while preserving the original MoE architecture. Furthermore, we organize the routing input into a 2D topographic map, spatially grouping neighboring elements. This structure enables MoGE to capture representations invariant to minor transformations, thereby significantly enhancing expert diversity and specialization. Comprehensive evaluations across various Transformer models for image classification and language modeling tasks demonstrate that MoGE substantially outperforms its MoE counterpart, with minimal additional memory and computation overhead. Our approach provides a simple yet effective solution to scale the number of experts and reduce redundancy among them. The source code is included in the supplementary material and will be publicly released.
zh

[NLP-104] MiMIC: Multi-Modal Indian Earnings Calls Dataset to Predict Stock Prices

【速读】： 本文旨在解决利用企业财报电话会议信息预测股市价格波动这一长期挑战。传统方法难以有效整合多样化的信息源，而本文提出的关键解决方案是构建一个多模态预测模型（Multimodal Predictive Model）。该模型通过整合来自财报电话会议文本转录（earnings call transcripts）、配套演示文稿中的图像与表格等多模态数据，结合基本面和技术指标等量化变量，实现了对财报电话会议后首个交易日股票价格走势的预测。为支持这一研究，作者创建了MiMIC（多模态印度财报电话会议）数据集，并采用了一种将文本与视觉模态的预测信号与定量变量相结合的多模态分析框架，从而实现全面的特征表征与分析。这种多模态方法的核心在于有效融合多种信息来源以提升金融预测准确性，为计算经济学领域的进一步研究提供了重要参考。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09257
作者: Sohom Ghosh,Arnab Maji,Sudip Kumar Naskar
机构: Jadavpur University ( Jadavpur University ); Independent Researcher ( Independent Researcher )
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Code and Dataset: this https URL

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Predicting stock market prices following corporate earnings calls remains a significant challenge for investors and researchers alike, requiring innovative approaches that can process diverse information sources. This study investigates the impact of corporate earnings calls on stock prices by introducing a multi-modal predictive model. We leverage textual data from earnings call transcripts, along with images and tables from accompanying presentations, to forecast stock price movements on the trading day immediately following these calls. To facilitate this research, we developed the MiMIC (Multi-Modal Indian Earnings Calls) dataset, encompassing companies representing the Nifty 50, Nifty MidCap 50, and Nifty Small 50 indices. The dataset includes earnings call transcripts, presentations, fundamentals, technical indicators, and subsequent stock prices. We present a multimodal analytical framework that integrates quantitative variables with predictive signals derived from textual and visual modalities, thereby enabling a holistic approach to feature representation and analysis. This multi-modal approach demonstrates the potential for integrating diverse information sources to enhance financial forecasting accuracy. To promote further research in computational economics, we have made the MiMIC dataset publicly available under the CC-NC-SA-4.0 licence. Our work contributes to the growing body of literature on market reactions to corporate communications and highlights the efficacy of multi-modal machine learning techniques in financial analysis.
zh

[NLP-105] Feature-Aware Malicious Output Detection and Mitigation

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决大型语言模型（Large Language Models, LLMs）在应对恶意内容（如毒性词汇）时缺乏有效防御机制的问题，特别是针对绕过模型安全限制的“越狱攻击”（jailbreak）。论文的关键解决方案是提出了一种特征感知的有害响应拒绝方法（Feature-aware Malicious Response Rejection, FMM）。该方法通过在模型的特征空间中检测潜在的恶意特征，并自适应调整模型的拒绝机制来实现。具体而言，FMM 在解码阶段利用一个简单的判别器检测潜在的恶意特性，并在识别到毒性标记时重新生成当前令牌。此外，通过激活修补（activation patching）技术，在后续令牌生成过程中引入额外的拒绝向量，引导模型拒绝有害输出。实验结果表明，FMM 方法能够在保持模型标准生成能力的同时，有效应对多种语言模型和攻击技术。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09191
作者: Weilong Dong,Peiguang Li,Yu Tian,Xinyi Zeng,Fengdi Li,Sirui Wang
机构: 未知
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The rapid advancement of large language models (LLMs) has brought significant benefits to various domains while introducing substantial risks. Despite being fine-tuned through reinforcement learning, LLMs lack the capability to discern malicious content, limiting their defense against jailbreak. To address these safety concerns, we propose a feature-aware method for harmful response rejection (FMM), which detects the presence of malicious features within the model’s feature space and adaptively adjusts the model’s rejection mechanism. By employing a simple discriminator, we detect potential malicious traits during the decoding phase. Upon detecting features indicative of toxic tokens, FMM regenerates the current token. By employing activation patching, an additional rejection vector is incorporated during the subsequent token generation, steering the model towards a refusal response. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach across multiple language models and diverse attack techniques, while crucially maintaining the models’ standard generation capabilities.
zh

[NLP-106] Parameterized Synthetic Text Generation with SimpleStories

【速读】： 该论文试图解决如何在大规模合成文本生成中同时实现简单性和多样性的问题。TinyStories 数据集存在的一些局限性为此研究提供了切入点。论文的关键解决方案在于提出了一种通过多层级抽象特征参数化提示的方法，这种方法能够系统性地控制故事的特性，从而确保语法和语义上的广泛多样性，最终构建了一个包含 200 万篇故事的 SimpleStories 数据集，分别用英语和日语编写。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09184
作者: Lennart Finke,Thomas Dooms,Mat Allen,Juan Diego Rodriguez,Noa Nabeshima,Dan Braun
机构: ETH Zürich; University of Antwerp (安特卫普大学); Dioptra; The University of Texas at Austin (德克萨斯大学奥斯汀分校); Independent; Apollo Research
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We present SimpleStories, a large synthetic story dataset in simple language, consisting of 2 million stories each in English and Japanese. Our method employs parametrization of prompts with features at multiple levels of abstraction, allowing for systematic control over story characteristics to ensure broad syntactic and semantic diversity. Building on and addressing limitations in the TinyStories dataset, our approach demonstrates that simplicity and variety can be achieved simultaneously in synthetic text generation at scale.
zh

[NLP-107] Langformers: Unified NLP Pipelines for Language Models

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决使用基于 Transformer 的语言模型进行自然语言处理（NLP）任务时面临的复杂性问题，包括多框架切换、重复性代码编写等挑战，这些问题可能阻碍非程序员、初学者以及甚至有经验的开发者快速构建原型。为了解决这些问题，论文提出了一种名为 Langformers 的开源 Python 库，其核心创新点在于通过统一且基于工厂模式的接口简化 NLP 管道设计，支持大语言模型（LLM）和掩码语言模型（MLM）任务。Langformers 将对话 AI、预训练掩码任务、文本分类、句子嵌入/重排序、数据标注、语义搜索及知识蒸馏等功能整合到一个连贯的 API 中，并兼容 Hugging Face 和 Ollama 等主流平台。其关键解决方案包括：(1) 面向特定任务的工厂类以抽象训练、推理和部署的复杂性；(2) 内置记忆与流式处理功能以增强对话代理能力；(3) 轻量级模块化设计以提升易用性。文档地址详见上述链接。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09170
作者: Rabindra Lamsal,Maria Rodriguez Read,Shanika Karunasekera
机构: The University of Melbourne (墨尔本大学)
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Transformer-based language models have revolutionized the field of natural language processing (NLP). However, using these models often involves navigating multiple frameworks and tools, as well as writing repetitive boilerplate code. This complexity can discourage non-programmers and beginners, and even slow down prototyping for experienced developers. To address these challenges, we introduce Langformers, an open-source Python library designed to streamline NLP pipelines through a unified, factory-based interface for large language model (LLM) and masked language model (MLM) tasks. Langformers integrates conversational AI, MLM pretraining, text classification, sentence embedding/reranking, data labelling, semantic search, and knowledge distillation into a cohesive API, supporting popular platforms such as Hugging Face and Ollama. Key innovations include: (1) task-specific factories that abstract training, inference, and deployment complexities; (2) built-in memory and streaming for conversational agents; and (3) lightweight, modular design that prioritizes ease of use. Documentation: this https URL
zh

[NLP-108] Can postgraduate translation students identify machine-generated text?

【速读】： 该论文试图解决的问题是评估受过语言训练的个体（如翻译学生）在辨别机器生成文本（ST）与人类撰写文本（HT）方面的能力，并探讨生成式人工智能（Generative AI）作为多语言内容创作工具的可行性。论文的关键解决方案在于通过短暂的训练让参与者识别合成文本中常见的文本异常特征，并要求他们对文本片段进行来源判断，从而量化其区分能力。研究发现，尽管参与者能够识别某些特定的文本异常，但整体上难以有效区分HT和ST，这表明现有训练方法存在不足，并提示需要进一步优化训练方案以及探索AI生成文本自然化编辑的必要性。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09164
作者: Michael Farrell
机构: 未知
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注: 10 pages, accepted for MT Summit 2025, Geneva, Switzerland, 23-27 June 2025

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Given the growing use of generative artificial intelligence as a tool for creating multilingual content and bypassing both machine and traditional translation methods, this study explores the ability of linguistically trained individuals to discern machine-generated output from human-written text (HT). After brief training sessions on the textual anomalies typically found in synthetic text (ST), twenty-three postgraduate translation students analysed excerpts of Italian prose and assigned likelihood scores to indicate whether they believed they were human-written or AI-generated (ChatGPT-4o). The results show that, on average, the students struggled to distinguish between HT and ST, with only two participants achieving notable accuracy. Closer analysis revealed that the students often identified the same textual anomalies in both HT and ST, although features such as low burstiness and self-contradiction were more frequently associated with ST. These findings suggest the need for improvements in the preparatory training. Moreover, the study raises questions about the necessity of editing synthetic text to make it sound more human-like and recommends further research to determine whether AI-generated text is already sufficiently natural-sounding not to require further refinement.
zh

[NLP-109] Efficient and Asymptotically Unbiased Constrained Decoding for Large Language Models

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决在大型语言模型的实际应用中，受限解码（constrained decoding）效率低下且可能导致输出分布引入无意偏倚的问题。传统基于前缀树（prefix-tree）的受限解码方法在基于GPU的模型推理范式下效率较低，并可能影响输出的公平性。为了解决这些问题，论文提出了一种名为Dynamic Importance Sampling for Constrained Decoding (DISC) 的新算法，其关键在于结合基于GPU的并行前缀验证（Parallel Prefix-Verification, PPV），通过动态重要性采样（dynamic importance sampling）实现理论上保证的渐近无偏性（asymptotic unbiasedness），同时克服了前缀树方法的低效问题。实验结果表明，该方法在效率和输出质量方面均优于现有方法。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09135
作者: Haotian Ye,Himanshu Jain,Chong You,Ananda Theertha Suresh,Haowei Lin,James Zou,Felix Yu
机构: 未知
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In real-world applications of large language models, outputs are often required to be confined: selecting items from predefined product or document sets, generating phrases that comply with safety standards, or conforming to specialized formatting styles. To control the generation, constrained decoding has been widely adopted. However, existing prefix-tree-based constrained decoding is inefficient under GPU-based model inference paradigms, and it introduces unintended biases into the output distribution. This paper introduces Dynamic Importance Sampling for Constrained Decoding (DISC) with GPU-based Parallel Prefix-Verification (PPV), a novel algorithm that leverages dynamic importance sampling to achieve theoretically guaranteed asymptotic unbiasedness and overcomes the inefficiency of prefix-tree. Extensive experiments demonstrate the superiority of our method over existing methods in both efficiency and output quality. These results highlight the potential of our methods to improve constrained generation in applications where adherence to specific constraints is essential.
zh

[NLP-110] VisuoThink: Empowering LVLM Reasoning with Multimodal Tree Search

【速读】： 该论文致力于解决现有大型视觉-语言模型在复杂推理任务中的局限性，这些任务通常需要人类通过视觉辅助和逐步思考来完成。传统方法主要依赖文本驱动的慢速思维或简单的视觉支持，但未能充分捕捉人类视觉-语言推理过程中复杂的交互特性。为克服这些限制，论文提出了一种名为VisuoThink的新框架，其关键是将视觉空间与语言领域无缝整合，通过渐进式的视觉-文本推理促进多模态慢速思维，并借助预测时的前瞻树搜索实现推理扩展。实验结果表明，VisuoThink在无需微调的情况下显著提升了几何和空间推理任务中的推理能力，达到了当前最先进的性能水平。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09130
作者: Yikun Wang,Siyin Wang,Qinyuan Cheng,Zhaoye Fei,Liang Ding,Qipeng Guo,Dacheng Tao,Xipeng Qiu
机构: Fudan University (复旦大学); Shanghai Innovation Institute (上海创新研究院); The University of Sydney (悉尼大学); Shanghai AI Laboratory (上海人工智能实验室); Nanyang Technological University (南洋理工大学)
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 12 pages

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Recent advancements in Large Vision-Language Models have showcased remarkable capabilities. However, they often falter when confronted with complex reasoning tasks that humans typically address through visual aids and deliberate, step-by-step thinking. While existing methods have explored text-based slow thinking or rudimentary visual assistance, they fall short of capturing the intricate, interleaved nature of human visual-verbal reasoning processes. To overcome these limitations and inspired by the mechanisms of slow thinking in human cognition, we introduce VisuoThink, a novel framework that seamlessly integrates visuospatial and linguistic domains. VisuoThink facilitates multimodal slow thinking by enabling progressive visual-textual reasoning and incorporates test-time scaling through look-ahead tree search. Extensive experiments demonstrate that VisuoThink significantly enhances reasoning capabilities via inference-time scaling, even without fine-tuning, achieving state-of-the-art performance in tasks involving geometry and spatial reasoning.
zh

[NLP-111] Optimizing FDTD Solvers for Electromagnetics: A Compiler-Guided Approach with High-Level Tensor Abstractions

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决传统有限差分时域（Finite Difference Time Domain, FDTD）方法在实现过程中因平台特定手工编码导致的可移植性差、开发开销大以及性能瓶颈等问题，限制其在现代硬件架构上的可扩展性。论文的关键解决方案是提出了一种基于MLIR/LLVM基础设施的端到端领域专用编译器，用于FDTD仿真。通过将三维FDTD内核表示为三维张量抽象上的操作，并结合显式的计算语义，该编译器能够自动应用高级优化技术（如循环分块、融合和向量化）。实验证明，与基于Python和NumPy的基线实现相比，该方法在Intel、AMD和ARM平台上实现了高达10倍的加速。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09118
作者: Yifei He,Måns I. Andersson,Stefano Markidis
机构: 未知
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The Finite Difference Time Domain (FDTD) method is a widely used numerical technique for solving Maxwell’s equations, particularly in computational electromagnetics and photonics. It enables accurate modeling of wave propagation in complex media and structures but comes with significant computational challenges. Traditional FDTD implementations rely on handwritten, platform-specific code that optimizes certain kernels while underperforming in others. The lack of portability increases development overhead and creates performance bottlenecks, limiting scalability across modern hardware architectures. To address these challenges, we introduce an end-to-end domain-specific compiler based on the MLIR/LLVM infrastructure for FDTD simulations. Our approach generates efficient and portable code optimized for diverse hardware this http URL implement the three-dimensional FDTD kernel as operations on a 3D tensor abstraction with explicit computational semantics. High-level optimizations such as loop tiling, fusion, and vectorization are automatically applied by the compiler. We evaluate our customized code generation pipeline on Intel, AMD, and ARM platforms, achieving up to 10\times speedup over baseline Python implementation using NumPy.
zh

[NLP-112] A Short Survey on Small Reasoning Models: Training Inference Applications and Research Directions

【速读】： 该论文试图解决大型推理模型（Large Reasoning Models, LRMs）在复杂推理任务中因计算需求过高而导致的效率瓶颈问题。论文的关键解决方案在于探索小型推理模型（Small Reasoning Models, SRMs），这些模型通常通过蒸馏技术从大型模型中获得，并以其更高的效率和独特的推理能力及认知路径为特点。论文通过对近170篇相关文献的综述，系统分析了SRMs的训练与推理技术，并探讨了其在特定领域的应用潜力及未来研究方向，旨在为研究人员提供高效实现高级推理功能的重要参考。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09100
作者: Chengyu Wang,Taolin Zhang,Richang Hong,Jun Huang
机构: Alibaba Cloud Computing (阿里云); School of Computer Science and Information Engineering, Hefei University of Technology (合肥工业大学)
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Recently, the reasoning capabilities of large reasoning models (LRMs), such as DeepSeek-R1, have seen significant advancements through the slow thinking process. Despite these achievements, the substantial computational demands of LRMs present considerable challenges. In contrast, small reasoning models (SRMs), often distilled from larger ones, offer greater efficiency and can exhibit distinct capabilities and cognitive trajectories compared to LRMs. This work surveys around 170 recently published papers on SRMs for tackling various complex reasoning tasks. We review the current landscape of SRMs and analyze diverse training and inference techniques related to SRMs. Furthermore, we provide a comprehensive review of SRMs for domain-specific applications and discuss possible future research directions. This survey serves as an essential reference for researchers to leverage or develop SRMs for advanced reasoning functionalities with high efficiency.
zh

[NLP-113] Enhancing Dialogue Systems with Discourse-Level Understanding Using Deep Canonical Correlation Analysis

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决现有对话模型在捕捉和利用长期对话历史方面的局限性问题。为应对这一挑战，论文提出了一种新颖的框架，通过引入深度典型相关分析（Deep Canonical Correlation Analysis, DCCA）实现话语级理解。该框架的关键在于学习话语标记（discourse tokens），以捕获话语片段与其周围上下文之间的关系，从而更好地建模长期依赖性。实验结果表明，在Ubuntu Dialogue Corpus上的响应选择任务中，基于改进的自动评估指标，该方法显著提升了性能，凸显了DCCA在过滤无关上下文、保留关键话语信息以实现更精确响应检索方面的潜力。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09094
作者: Akanksha Mehndiratta,Krishna Asawa
机构: 未知
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The evolution of conversational agents has been driven by the need for more contextually aware systems that can effectively manage dialogue over extended interactions. To address the limitations of existing models in capturing and utilizing long-term conversational history, we propose a novel framework that integrates Deep Canonical Correlation Analysis (DCCA) for discourse-level understanding. This framework learns discourse tokens to capture relationships between utterances and their surrounding context, enabling a better understanding of long-term dependencies. Experiments on the Ubuntu Dialogue Corpus demonstrate significant enhancement in response selection, based on the improved automatic evaluation metric scores. The results highlight the potential of DCCA in improving dialogue systems by allowing them to filter out irrelevant context and retain critical discourse information for more accurate response retrieval.
zh

[NLP-114] A Multi-view Discourse Framework for Integrating Semantic and Syntactic Features in Dialog Agents

【速读】： 该论文旨在解决现有基于检索的多轮对话系统中响应选择方法忽视话语上下文交互或同等对待所有utterances的问题。论文提出了一种基于语篇意识的框架，其关键是通过两步方法整合语义和句法特征以构建话语级理解：首先利用多视图典型相关分析（Multi-view Canonical Correlation Analysis, MCCA）编码每个utterance和response的上下文、位置及句法特征；然后通过典型相关分析（Canonical Correlation Analysis, CCA）在共享子空间中学习话语token，捕捉utterance与其周围轮次之间的关系。实验结果表明，该模型在Ubuntu Dialogue Corpus上的自动评估指标中取得了显著改进。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09073
作者: Akanksha Mehndiratta,Krishna Asawa
机构: 未知
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Multiturn dialogue models aim to generate human-like responses by leveraging conversational context, consisting of utterances from previous exchanges. Existing methods often neglect the interactions between these utterances or treat all of them as equally significant. This paper introduces a discourse-aware framework for response selection in retrieval-based dialogue systems. The proposed model first encodes each utterance and response with contextual, positional, and syntactic features using Multi-view Canonical Correlation Analysis (MCCA). It then learns discourse tokens that capture relationships between an utterance and its surrounding turns in a shared subspace via Canonical Correlation Analysis (CCA). This two-step approach effectively integrates semantic and syntactic features to build discourse-level understanding. Experiments on the Ubuntu Dialogue Corpus demonstrate that our model achieves significant improvements in automatic evaluation metrics, highlighting its effectiveness in response selection.
zh

[NLP-115] Exploration of Plan-Guided Summarization for Narrative Texts: the Case of Small Language Models NAACL2025

【速读】： 该论文试图解决小语言模型（Small Language Models, SLMs）在长文档叙事任务中的摘要生成问题，特别是如何通过基于计划的引导方法减少幻觉（hallucinations），同时提高摘要的忠实性（faithfulness）。论文的关键在于分析和比较两种基于计划的方案：一种是针对细粒度细节（如日期或命名实体）的现有方法；另一种是作者提出的更高层次的、基于叙事的计划制定方法。研究结果表明，无论采用哪种计划引导方法，在摘要质量和忠实性方面均未显著优于无计划引导的基线模型。此外，虽然基于计划的摘要通常与计划保持一致，但计划本身同样容易包含幻觉，导致最终生成的摘要仍然不够忠实。因此，论文对基于计划的摘要方法提出了警示，尤其是在处理长且复杂的叙事文本领域。

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09071
作者: Matt Grenander,Siddharth Varia,Paula Czarnowska,Yogarshi Vyas,Kishaloy Halder,Bonan Min
机构: AWS AI Labs; School of Informatics, University of Edinburgh (爱丁堡大学信息学院); PolyAI
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: Accepted to the 7th Workshop on Narrative Understanding (WNU), co-located with NAACL 2025

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Plan-guided summarization attempts to reduce hallucinations in small language models (SLMs) by grounding generated summaries to the source text, typically by targeting fine-grained details such as dates or named entities.
